sections.label: Section 1126
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστιν , ἀλλὰ τήνδ ’ ὁρᾷς δάμαρτα σήν .
sections.translation: No , no ; ’ tis thy own wife thou seest here .

sections.label: Section 1127
sections.original: ὅρα γε μή τι φάσμα νερτέρων τόδ ’ ᾖ .
sections.translation: Beware it be not a phantom from that nether world .

sections.label: Section 1128
sections.original: οὐ ψυχαγωγὸν τόνδ ’ ἐποιήσω ξένον .
sections.translation: No necromancer was this guest whom thou didst welcome .

sections.label: Section 1129
sections.original: ἀλλ ’ ἣν ἔθαπτον εἰσορῶ δάμαρτ ’ ἐμήν ;
sections.translation: Do I behold my wife , her whom I buried ?

sections.label: Section 1130
sections.original: σάφ ’ ἴσθʼ . ἀπιστεῖν δ ’ οὔ σε θαυμάζω τύχην .
sections.translation: Be well assured thereof ; still I marvel not thou dost distrust thy luck .

sections.label: Section 1131
sections.original: θίγω , προσείπω ζῶσαν ὡς δάμαρτ ’ ἐμήν ;
sections.translation: May I touch her , may I speak to her as my living wife ?

sections.label: Section 1132
sections.original: πρόσειπʼ . ἔχεις γὰρ πᾶν ὅσονπερ ἤθελες .
sections.translation: Speak to her . For thou hast all thy heart ’ s desire .

sections.label: Section 1133
sections.original: ὦ φιλτάτης γυναικὸς ὄμμα καὶ δέμας ,
sections.translation: O form and features of my well-loved wife ! past all hope I hold thee , never expecting to see thee again .

sections.label: Section 1135
sections.original: ἔχεις · φθόνος δὲ μὴ γένοιτό τις θεῶν .
sections.translation: So thou dost ; may no jealous god rise against thee !

sections.label: Section 1136
sections.original: ὦ τοῦ μεγίστου Ζηνὸς εὐγενὲς τέκνον ,
sections.translation: O noble son of almighty Zeus , good luck to thee ! may the father that begat thee hold thee in his keeping ; for thou and none else hast raised my fallen fortunes . How didst thou bring her from the world below to this light of day ?

sections.label: Section 1140
sections.original: μάχην συνάψας δαιμόνων τῷ κυρίῳ .
sections.translation: By encountering the god [*] who had her in his power .

sections.label: Section 1141
sections.original: ποῦ τόνδε Θανάτῳ φῂς ἀγῶνα συμβαλεῖν ;
sections.translation: Where didst thou engage with Death ? tell me this .

sections.label: Section 1142
sections.original: τύμβον παρ ’ αὐτὸν ἐκ λόχου μάρψας χεροῖν .
sections.translation: Just by the tomb I from my ambush sprang and caught him in my grip .

sections.label: Section 1143
sections.original: τί γάρ ποθ ’ ἥδ ’ ἄναυδος ἕστηκεν γυνή ;
sections.translation: But why thus speechless stands my wife ?

sections.label: Section 1144
sections.original: οὔπω θέμις σοι τῆσδε προσφωνημάτων
sections.translation: ’ Tis not lawful yet for thee to hear her speak ,

sections.label: Section 1145
sections.original: κλύειν , πρὶν ἂν θεοῖσι τοῖσι νερτέροις
sections.translation: ere she be purified from the gods below and the third day be come . So lead her in ; and hereafter , e ’ en as now , be just and kind to guests , Admetus . Now farewell ! for I must go to perform my appointed task

sections.label: Section 1150
sections.original: Σθενέλου τυράννῳ παιδὶ πορσυνῶ μολών .
sections.translation: for the lordly son of Sthenelus .

sections.label: Section 1151
sections.original: μεῖνον παρ ’ ἡμῖν καὶ ξυνέστιος γενοῦ .
sections.translation: Abide with us and be our welcome guest .

sections.label: Section 1152
sections.original: αὖθις τόδ ’ ἔσται , νῦν δ ’ ἐπείγεσθαί με δεῖ .
sections.translation: Another time ; now must I use all haste .

sections.label: Section 1153
sections.original: ἀλλ ’ εὐτυχοίης , νόστιμον δ ’ ἔλθοις δόμον .
sections.translation: Good luck to thee ! and mayst thou come again ! To the citizens and ail my realm

sections.label: Section 1155
sections.original: χοροὺς ἐπ ’ ἐσθλαῖς συμφοραῖσιν ἱστάναι
sections.translation: I make this proclamation , that they institute dances in honour of the glad event , and make the altars steam with sacrifice , and offer prayers ; for now have I moored my bark of life in a happier haven than before , and so will own myself a happy man .

sections.label: Section 1159
sections.original: πολλαὶ μορφαὶ τῶν δαιμονίων ,
sections.translation: Many are the shapes that fortune takes ,

sections.label: Section 1160
sections.original: πολλὰ δ ’ ἀέλπτως κραίνουσι θεοί ·
sections.translation: and oft the gods bring things to pass beyond our expectation . That which we deemed so sure is not fulfilled , while for that we never thought would be , God finds out a way . And such hath been the issue in the present case .
